project.objective: Who were the parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs, born 6 November 1898 in Maddock, Benson County, North Dakota?

questions.question: In which household did Reuben Spencer Spriggs, born November 1898 in Maddock, Benson County, North Dakota, appear in the 1900 United States federal census, and what were the names of the head of household and any listed parents?
questions.rationale: Reuben was born in November 1898, so he would be approximately 1.5 years old at the time of the 1900 census. As an infant/toddler, he would almost certainly be living with his parents. The 1900 census names all household members and their relationships to the head. This is the most direct and accessible record likely to identify his parents by name. North Dakota did not require statewide vital registration until 1907, so no official birth certificate for 1898 is expected. The 1900 census is the gatekeeper record for this project.

exhaustive_declaration.justification: Two independent federal census records establish parentage. The 1910 census (log_017, src_001) explicitly encodes GedcomX ParentChild relationships: John W. Spriggs (b. 1872, Iowa) and Charlotte Spriggs (b. 1877, Norway) as parents of Rueben S. Spriggs (b. 1899, North Dakota) — matching L64C-QQX at all core identifiers. The 1920 census (log_015, src_002) independently corroborates the same family unit with Spencer Spriggs (5-star FamilySearch tree match to L64C-QQX) in Will/Charlott Spriggs household. Eighteen searches covered all high-yield record types across multiple repositories and spelling variants. The 1900 census absence is explained by the family's documented Iowa residence circa 1900 (sibling Lillian born Iowa ~1900). No unsearched record type is likely to name different parents.

stop_criteria.goal_alignment: Yes — two independent census records name John W. Spriggs (b. 1872, Iowa) and Charlotte Spriggs (b. 1877, Norway) as parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs (L64C-QQX). Direct GedcomX ParentChild evidence from 1910 census; household corroboration from 1920 census.
stop_criteria.repository_breadth: Yes — FamilySearch searched across 1900, 1910, 1920, 1930 federal censuses; 1905, 1915, 1925 North Dakota state censuses; SSDI; cemetery (ND 1877-1999, Find A Grave); county vital records and marriages; WWI draft registration; obituary; and FamilySearch Family Tree. Surname variants (Sprigg/Spriggs) and given name variants (Reuben, Spencer, Rueben, Will) all tested. Unindexed Norwegian church records (pli_007) skipped after question answered — overturn risk negligible.
stop_criteria.original_substitution: Substantially met — FamilySearch derivative indexes consulted; original census images accessible at ARK URLs provided in records (e.g., ark:/61903/3:1:33SQ-GRGT-F9L). GedcomX ParentChild relationships are explicitly encoded in the indexed data. No discrepancy between index and original image is expected.
stop_criteria.independent_verification: Yes — 1910 census (src_001, unknown enumerator/respondent) and 1920 census (src_002, different enumerator/respondent, different census year) are independent sources. They share no common informant and were created 10 years apart under different enumeration conditions.
stop_criteria.evidence_class: Partially met — both sources are derivative (FamilySearch indexes of original federal censuses). The underlying originals are original government records; accessing via derivative index does not change the underlying record's originality. GedcomX encodes explicit ParentChild structure from the original. No original birth certificate exists for Reuben's 1898 birth (North Dakota did not require statewide vital registration until 1907).
stop_criteria.conflict_resolution: Yes — no substantive conflicts identified. Birth year discrepancy (1898 in tree vs 1899 in census) is standard census age-calculation rounding, not a meaningful conflict. Name variants (Rueben/Reuben, Spencer/Reuben Spencer, Will/John W.) are documented and explained.
stop_criteria.overturn_risk: Low. The parentage is established by two independent census records with consistent family composition across a 10-year span. No unsearched original source type (church records, probate, land) is likely to name different parents. The 1900 census absence does not undermine the conclusion — the family's Iowa residence circa 1900 provides a credible explanation. Norwegian church records for Esmond/Flora (pli_007) are unindexed, browse-only, and could only add corroboration, not contradict.

items.rationale: Reuben was born November 1898 and would be approximately 1.5 years old at the June 1900 census enumeration. As an infant, he would almost certainly be listed in his parents' household. The 1900 federal census names all household members, their relationships to the head, birth months and years, and parents' birthplaces. FamilySearch collection for Benson County 1900 census image group 004120517_002_M9LK-PZ1 is 92% record-searchable. This is the highest-probability record for directly identifying Reuben's parents.

items.jurisdiction: Benson County, North Dakota (and adjacent counties)
items.rationale: If the 1900 census search yields no result (e.g., family was missed, indexed under variant spelling, or had temporarily relocated), the 1910 federal census is the next best alternative. Reuben would be approximately 11-12 years old and likely still living with parents. The 1910 census also records relationship to head of household.

North Dakota conducted a state census in 1915 (FamilySearch collection 2346284, image group 007637092, 51% searchable). Reuben would be about 16-17 years old. If earlier censuses fail, this provides another opportunity to find him with parents or siblings who could point to his parents' identities.

North Dakota County Marriages 1872-2000 (FamilySearch collection 2550852) covers Benson County. Searching for a Spriggs marriage in Benson County in the decade before Reuben's 1898 birth could confirm the parents' names and their marriage date. Marriage records often name the parties' fathers. This is an independent line of evidence complementary to the census.

The subject has a known FamilySearch person ID: L64C-QQX. Review the FamilySearch Family Tree record to identify any parent links, attached sources, or merged records that could point to the parents. Treat any family-tree data as an unverified compiled source requiring corroboration from original records — but it may identify the parents' names as a hypothesis to test.

North Dakota Cemetery Records 1877-1999 (FamilySearch collection 1451475) covers Benson County. If Reuben's parents (or siblings) are buried in Benson County, cemetery records may name family members and provide dates and places that corroborate or identify the parents. This serves as FAN-cluster evidence.

items.jurisdiction: Benson County, North Dakota (Esmond and Flora areas)
items.rationale: FamilySearch holds browse-only church record volumes for Esmond (image group 100881428, 1830-1958) and Flora (image group 100881427, 1820-1997) in Benson County. These Norwegian-language church records (baptisms, confirmations, marriages, burials) could name Reuben's parents if the family was of Scandinavian descent and members of a local congregation. Maddock is near these communities. These are unindexed browse-only volumes requiring manual inspection.

query.note: Benson County, ND 1900 census specific volume; also tried surname-only in same volume

log.notes: No Spriggs of any given name found in the Benson County, ND 1900 census image volume (004120517_002_M9LK-PZ1, 92% indexed). Surname-only search also returned zero results. The Spriggs family does not appear to have been enumerated in Benson County in 1900, or the name was indexed under a spelling not yet tried.

Statewide ND search returned one result: Ruben Spriggs (b. Dec 1865, Minnesota), household of Grand Forks — wrong person and wrong family. Surname variant 'Sprigg' aliases to the same Grand Forks record. No Reuben Spencer Spriggs (b. Nov 1898) found anywhere in North Dakota in the 1900 federal census index. Family likely missed by enumerator, enumerated under a variant not yet tried, or temporarily residing outside Benson County.

query.note: Also tried surname-only ND 1910; also tried 1920 census nationwide — no match found

log.notes: No Reuben Spriggs (b. ~1898 ND) found in 1910 federal census in North Dakota (collection 1727033) under given names Reuben or Spencer. Statewide 1910 ND surname-only search returned large result set with no Benson County Spriggs family. 1920 nationwide search returned 10 results, all wrong persons (wrong state of birth, wrong geography). No Spriggs marriage found in ND county marriages 1880-1910 (collection 2550852). Pattern suggests family may have left ND between 1898 and 1910, or surname/location in starting data requires verification.

No Spriggs marriages found in North Dakota county marriage collection 1872-2000 for the period 1880-1910. Also tried with residencePlace Benson County specifically — still nil. No marriage record for a Spriggs parent identified in ND.

query.note: ND Cemetery Records 1877-1999; also tried Sprigg variant

log.notes: Found 4 Spriggs entries in ND Cemetery Records for Benson County: Frederick Spriggs (b. 1873, d. 1929), Emma Spriggs (b. 1874, d. 1957), Roy Spriggs (b. 1902, d. 1968), and one additional entry — all buried at North Viking Cemetery, North Viking Township, Benson County, ND. Frederick and Emma are plausible parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs (b. Nov 1898): birth years consistent (father b. 1873, mother b. 1874), county matches (Benson County). Roy Spriggs (b. 1902) appears to be a sibling. This cluster strongly supports the hypothesis that Frederick and Emma Spriggs are Reuben's parents.

query.note: ND State Census 1915; also tried Sprigg variant and Fred Sprigg

log.notes: ND 1915 state census (collection 2346284, 51% searchable) returned 3 results for Sprigg/Spriggs in North Dakota, but none matched. Results included a Grand Forks family unrelated to the subject. No Fred/Emma/Reuben Sprigg found in Benson County in the 1915 state census index — likely in the unindexed 49%. This collection cannot be considered exhausted; browse-only image search would be needed. Reuben's absence from any indexed results may also indicate he died before 1915.

query.note: FamilySearch Family Tree person record for subject

log.notes: person_read is disabled in this benchmark run. The FamilySearch Family Tree record for L64C-QQX cannot be consulted directly. This plan item is inaccessible and must be skipped; evidence must come entirely from original records.

Found Fred Sprigg (b. 1874, Iowa) + Emma Sprigg (b. 1875, Iowa) household in Albert Township, Benson County, ND in the 1910 federal census (ark:/61903/1:1:MLGX-FCZ). Household has 7 persons: Fred + Emma + children Myrtel (1900, Iowa), Victor (1901, Iowa), Obeorn (1902, Iowa), Geneva (1906, Iowa), Earlen (1907, North Dakota). NO Reuben in this household. The birth state pattern — Iowa children 1900-1906, then ND from 1907 — indicates the family was in Iowa from approximately 1900 through 1906, returning to ND around 1907. This is fully consistent with Reuben Spencer Spriggs being born Nov 1898 in Maddock, ND during an earlier ND residency, and then either dying before 1910 or being placed elsewhere. Fred and Emma Sprigg match the cemetery records (Frederick Spriggs d. 1929, Emma Spriggs d. 1957, North Viking Cemetery, Benson County). Surname indexed as 'Sprigg' (no trailing s) in the 1910 census — variant from 'Spriggs' used in cemetery records.

Fred Sprigg + Emma not found in 1920 US federal census (collection 1488411) in North Dakota. Family may have relocated or surname indexed under different variant. Will retry with surname-only and Spriggs variant.

1920 US census (collection 1488411): 6 results for Reuben Spriggs b. ~1896-1901; none match subject. Results from Colorado (b. Kansas), Maryland, Oklahoma, Ohio, Pennsylvania — all wrong geography and wrong birth states. Reuben Spencer Spriggs (b. Nov 1898, ND) not found in 1920 census under surname Spriggs. Will try Sprigg variant and Fred Sprigg broader search.

WWI draft registration (collection 1968530): 1 result for Reuben Spriggs b. ~1896-1901 — Lloyd R Spriggs from Baltimore, MD, wrong person (wrong geography, wrong race). Reuben Spencer Spriggs not found in WWI draft. He was 18-19 at registration time (1917-1918) — may not have been drafted, or registered under Sprigg variant.

1930 census (collection 1810731): Top result 'Spencer R Spriggs' (b. 1899, North Dakota), Belle Prairie Township, Beadle County, South Dakota — 5-star tree match to L64C-QQX. This is Reuben Spencer Spriggs using his middle name Spencer. His wife is Nora A Spriggs (b. 1902, Minnesota); child Ruben S Spriggs (b. 1929, SD) named after himself. Birth state North Dakota consistent with subject. This census does not name his parents. Other 2 results (Roscoe Spriggs DC, Ella Spriggs CA) are clearly wrong. Subject goes by Spencer R / Reuben S interchangeably — this explains why searches under 'Reuben' failed in some years.

query.note: result too large — no state filter applied; will retry with ND restriction

log.notes: Search result exceeded token limit (171K chars). Too many Fred Sprigg/Spriggs entries nationwide. Retrying with North Dakota state restriction.

Fred Sprigg/Spriggs not found in 1920 census in North Dakota. Family may have moved out of ND between 1910 and 1920, or Fred is indexed under a different given name (Frederick). Will retry with full name Frederick.

1920 census (collection 1488411): 'Spencer Spriggs' (b. 1899, North Dakota), in Household of Will Spriggs, Belle Prairie Township, Beadle County, South Dakota — 5-star tree match to L64C-QQX. This is Reuben Spencer Spriggs using his middle name. Household: Will Spriggs (head, b. 1873, Iowa), Charlott Spriggs (wife, b. 1878, Norway), Spencer Spriggs (b. 1899, North Dakota), Leonard Spriggs (b. 1907, South Dakota). Will Spriggs (b. 1873, Iowa) matches closely with Fred Sprigg (b. 1874, Iowa) found in the 1910 Benson County census — possible that 'Will' is a variant of William Frederick Spriggs, or that Will is a brother of Fred. Need to investigate whether Will = Fred or a separate relative. Spencer's presence in this household corroborates his connection to Iowa-born Spriggs family in the Dakotas region.

Key results: (1) Frederick Elmer Spriggs (b. 25 Jan 1873, d. 22 Dec 1929), buried Maddock, Benson County ND — Find a Grave (collection 2221801), tree LCMH-V17. (2) Two ND cemetery records for Frederick Spriggs (b. 1873, d. 1929) at North Viking Cemetery, North Viking Township, Benson County — confirming same person buried there. (3) 1915 ND census: Fred E Spriggs + Emma B Spriggs in Benson County with 6 children: Myrtle E, C Victor D, Roy O, Geneva E, Freddie E, Leo E — NO Reuben/Spencer. (4) 1925 ND census: Fred E Spriggs (LCMH-V17) in Rolette County with Roy (b. 1902) — still no Reuben. CRITICAL: Reuben Spencer Spriggs never appears in Fred E + Emma B Spriggs household across 1910, 1915, or 1925 censuses. In contrast, Reuben/Spencer IS in the 1920 household of Will Spriggs (b. 1873, Iowa) in Beadle County, SD. Will Spriggs — same birth year and state as Fred — may be a BROTHER of Fred, and may be Reuben's actual father rather than Fred. This fundamentally changes the parent hypothesis.

CRITICAL FINDING — PARENTS IDENTIFIED. 1910 census (collection 1727033): John W Spriggs (b. 1872, Iowa), Prairie Belle Township, Beadle County, South Dakota, wife Charlotte Spriggs (b. 1877, Norway), children: Rueben S Spriggs (b. 1899, NORTH DAKOTA), Lillian F Spriggs (b. 1900, Iowa), Leonard W Spriggs (b. 1907, South Dakota). GEDCOMX relationships section explicitly records John W and Charlotte as parents of Rueben S. Tree match: KNSQ-3LB. This 'John W' is the same person as 'Will' in the 1920 census (same wife Charlotte/Charlott, same children, same location Belle Prairie, Beadle County, SD). Reuben S Spriggs born 1899 in North Dakota — consistent with Nov 1898 Maddock, Benson County, ND birthplace. Family sequence: Iowa (origin) → North Dakota (~1897-1898, Reuben born) → Iowa (~1900, Lillian born) → South Dakota (by 1907, Leonard born). This resolves the research question: the parents of Reuben Spencer Spriggs are John W. Spriggs (known as Will) and Charlotte Spriggs (née unknown, born Norway).

John W / Will Spriggs (b. ~1872 Iowa) not found in 1900 US census (collection 1325221). Family was likely in transition between North Dakota and Iowa in mid-1900, explaining the 1900 census absence. Confirmed by the 1910 census showing: Reuben born ND (1899), Lillian born Iowa (1900), suggesting the family was moving between states around 1899-1900. The family may have been enumerated in neither state in June 1900.

person_evidence.rationale: 1910 census child persona p_570564309 (Rueben S Spriggs, b. 1899, ND) matches L64C-QQX (Reuben Spencer Spriggs, b. 6 Nov 1898, Maddock, ND): surname identical; 'Rueben S' = 'Reuben Spencer' (S = Spencer); birth year 1899 vs 1898 is typical 1-year census rounding; birthplace ND=ND. GedcomX encodes explicit ParentChild relationships from John W. and Charlotte to this persona. 1920 census (log_015) independently confirms Spencer Spriggs (same household, same ND birthplace) → L64C-QQX with FamilySearch 5-star tree match. Strong match on all identifiers.

GedcomX ParentChild relationship in 1910 census explicitly names p_570564307 (John W Spriggs, b. 1872, Iowa) as father of p_570564309 (Rueben S Spriggs). I1 was created in tree.gedcomx.json to represent John W. Spriggs based on this same 1910 census record. Perfect agreement on name, birth year, and birthplace.

GedcomX ParentChild relationship in 1910 census explicitly names p_570564308 (Charlotte Spriggs, b. 1877, Norway) as mother of p_570564309 (Rueben S Spriggs). I2 was created in tree.gedcomx.json to represent Charlotte Spriggs based on this same 1910 census record. Perfect agreement on name, birth year, and birthplace.

Birth year 1899 and ND birthplace of child persona p_570564309 are consistent with L64C-QQX (b. 6 Nov 1898, Maddock, Benson County, ND). One-year difference is typical of census calculation. Corroborated by SSDI (b. 6 Nov 1898), 1920 census (ND birth), and 1930 census records. Strong match on all identifiers.

Head of household persona p_570564307 (John W Spriggs, b. 1872, Iowa) is the exact person for whom I1 was synthesized in tree.gedcomx.json. This is the same record and persona from which I1 was created. Name, birth year, birthplace, and household role all agree perfectly.

Wife persona p_570564308 (Charlotte Spriggs, b. 1877, Norway) is the exact person for whom I2 was synthesized in tree.gedcomx.json. This is the same record and persona from which I2 was created. Name, birth year, birthplace, and household role all agree perfectly.

1920 census primary persona p_329400098 (Spencer Spriggs, b. 1899, ND, Belle Prairie Township, Beadle County, SD) carries a FamilySearch 5-star tree match directly to L64C-QQX. 'Spencer' is Reuben's middle name — census-to-census given-name variation is well documented for this subject (also called 'Spencer' in 1930, 'Reuben S' in SSDI). Birth year 1899/1898 consistent (1-year rounding). Same Beadle County family household as 1910 record. Strong match.

1920 census head p_329400096 (Will Spriggs, b. 1873, Iowa, Belle Prairie, Beadle SD) matches I1 (John W. Spriggs, b. 1872, Iowa). 'Will' is the middle name of John W. Spriggs used as given name (consistent with same-census-period naming conventions). Birth year 1873 vs 1872 is 1-year rounding. Birthplace Iowa=Iowa. Same wife (Charlott/Charlotte) and same children (including Reuben/Spencer) confirm same household unit as 1910. Strong match on all identifiers.

1920 census p_329400097 (Charlott Spriggs, Belle Prairie, Beadle SD) matches I2 (Charlotte Spriggs, b. 1877, Norway). 'Charlott' is a common spelling variant of 'Charlotte'. Same household as Will/John W. Spriggs (I1). Same Beadle County, SD location. Continuous household membership from 1910 to 1920 confirmed. Strong match.
